meta.title: Private Verifiable AI in an Age of Confusion

transcript:
Hello Breakpoint Abu Dhabi, great to see everyone here. I hope you're doing well. Some really great presentations, some hard to follow up. I wanted to ask all of you a simple question, which is: Can you trust your AI infrastructure? You know, we're entering an agentic economy where agents are going to be in the middle of every transaction, and the thing powering those agents is, of course, a model. But I want to ask you, do you know that that model is running correctly? Do you know that it's hitting the benchmarks of intelligence that you would expect? Can you guarantee that every word of text or every pixel in an image that that model is producing is being rendered correctly? I would say those questions are of critical importance because if AI agents are our interface to the world鈥攊f they are our interface to wallets, to software, to economic opportunities鈥攖hen having a suddenly stupid AI agent, or an AI agent that is turned against its creators, is a huge issue.

You know, in crypto, we care about cryptoeconomic security; we audit smart contracts ten times. But in AI, I see a lot of people using Anthropic. Anthropic is a great company, but they also released Claude Code which was compromised in terms of its coding ability for over a month due to a problem in its inference engine. That impacted the usability of that product for everyone who used it for a long time, had a huge economic consequence for them. So I would say that verifiability around AI is very important, but it's not been provided traditionally. And the reason is that it's very difficult to do. Traditionally, it's been extremely expensive鈥攎ight cost you 10 to a thousand times more than unverified inference鈥攐r you make compromises. Like you take an optimistic approach where you just sort of assume that everything is working and then at some point you check and you penalize people if they've made a mistake. That works really well until you have an asymmetric risk situation where someone could earn ten million dollars by rugging your agent and could only be slashed ten thousand dollars. So there's a big security compromise there.

Also, people have tried TEEs (Trusted Execution Environments). And, you know, the problem we have in crypto is you can't base cryptoeconomic security on TEEs because they themselves are repeatedly compromised. So you can't make AI security based on TEEs either. It's good for privacy as a mitigation, but not for security. The other thing is that in crypto we don't have high-scale providers. It's really hard to put volume through the pipes because people have been focused on delivering a bazaar of models鈥攁 lot of different models that meet long-tail use cases鈥攁nd there's really been no focus on delivering one high-intelligence model at the kind of scale that would be useful to apps that, for example, suddenly get popular.

So that's what Ambient is. We would like to be your high-scale provider of verified machine intelligence, and we want to provide you that service for exactly the same price as unverified inference. We want to give you privacy options ranging from anonymity to full end-to-end encryption within TEEs without compromising the security of the verified inference, which again is providing you a guarantee that a model was run correctly... What that enables is provably fair economic games, which is, of course, what we'd like to play on Solana. We also offer some integrated capabilities that you find in like the OpenAIs of the world, like search and deep research. And again, we focus on high-scale delivery.

I'm going to just try a little demo here... This is our chat client, this is available today. We're asking it about the speed of Solana. It did all the blockchain things in the background. It already verified all of this text, proving that this text was rendered by a 16-bit quantization of GLM 4.6. Every word of that text was rendered correctly by that model... So you don't have to worry about whether your infrastructure provider is giving you incorrect results or results that aren't matching the published benchmarks. Another demo I can run in the background briefly... is research. So we have some additional capabilities. All of these capabilities are powered by the same verified intelligence engine... So, very briefly, we've got a Solana Quickstart on docs.ambient.xyz. It's available right now widely. Please check us out. We are ambient.xyz on the web. I am @IridiumEagle on X. You can check out our app.ambient.xyz. Please feel free to get in touch to get some free inference. We'd love to support your applications on Solana and the future of agentic commerce as a whole. Thank you very much.

# Solana Breakpoint 2025: Key Announcements and Stories from Saturday
Day Three maintained the high expectations with announcements from Solflare, Solana Mobile, and Colosseum, among others.
[Pablo](https://solanafloor.com/author/pablo)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
It was said that this year’s Breakpoint would be Solana’s biggest conference yet, and I’m sure none of the several thousand delegates that made their way to the Etihad Arena this week would disagree with that sentiment.
Saturday’s session featured a diverse set of updates highlighting Solana’s continued expansion across hardware, real-world assets, and institutional finance, as well as side events such as Predictify, hosted by[Kalshi](https://x.com/Kalshi?s=20).
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999766576091025438?s=20)> The last day of Breakpoint 2025 delivered a mix of hackathon results, product demonstrations, and strategic commitments that underscored the network’s growing role as a settlement layer for both digital and traditional assets.
Here’s what you missed if you’re not in Abu Dhabi:
## At Breakpoint
1. Colosseum has announced winners for its Cypherpunk hackathon
* *Unruggable, the Solana-native hardware wallet, is crowned grand champion.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999765402453467212?s=20)> 1. Uranium Digital demoed their product, which enables institutions to buy Uranium onchain for the first time.
* *Settlement is happening fully on Solana*
* *The entire crypto experience is abstracted*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999744679441826044?s=20)> 1. RockawayX launches 3 vaults on Solana.
* *Risk-curated RWA vault on Kamino*
* *Additional vaults on Exponent Finance and MidasRWA*
* *Partners with Figure, Onre, Solstice, and Huma as professional curators for these vaults.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999753859456204968?s=20)> 1. Solana Mobile is expanding beyond its own devices to additional hardware.
* *Working with FXTech, MediaTek, and Trustonic to integrate the Solana Mobile stack at the chipset level for other Android devices.*
* *MediaTek ships roughly 50% of all Android devices globally.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999757725505642967?s=20)> 1. Splyce Finance introduces $SFULC on Solana.
* *$SFULC is a yield-bearing token offering 10% APY powered by Fulcrum Lending*
* *Powered by real cash flows from senior, secured multifamily credit.*
* *$SFULC can be minted with stablecoins or bought directly on Raydium.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999761140931526825?s=20)> 1. VC Firm 6th Man Ventures announces that it is focused on Solan

# Jupiter Unveils JupUSD and a New Era of Onchain Finance at Breakpoint
JupUSD anchors Jupiter鈥檚 push toward integrated, compliant, and institutional-grade onchain finance on Solana
![Yasmin Werner](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/cropped-cropped-CCIcon-32x32.png)By[Yasmin Werner](https://coincentral.com/author/yasmin/)December 12, 2025**3 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/jupiter.jpg "Jupiter Unveils JupUSD and a New Era of Onchain Finance at Breakpoint")
## TLDR
* JupUSD becomes Jupiter鈥檚 core stablecoin across swaps, lending, perps, and strategies
* Jupiter Lend exits beta, boosting stablecoin efficiency with higher LTVs and lower penalties
* JupUSD shifts from standalone asset to infrastructure powering Solana-native DeFi
* KRW-pegged stablecoin signals Solana鈥檚 growing focus on regulated, institutional finance
* New data, verification, and trading tools support scalable, professional onchain markets
Jupiter announced a coordinated expansion of onchain finance capabilities at Breakpoint, centered on JupUSD and integrated infrastructure upgrades. The update combines stablecoins, lending, data, and trading into a single Solana-native stack. As a result, JupUSD anchors a broader push toward compliant, scalable, and professional onchain finance.
## JupUSD Expands Stablecoin Utility Across Jupiter
[Jupiter](https://coincentral.com/jupiter-targets-full-launch-of-prediction-market-platform-before-2026/)introduced JupUSD as a deeply integrated stablecoin designed to operate across its full product suite. The platform embedded JupUSD into swaps, perpetuals, lending, and automated strategies. Consequently, JupUSD supports consistent liquidity while sharing platform economics with active users.
JupUSD connects stable value with execution layers already processing billions in volume on Solana. Therefore, the design enables protocol-level coordination instead of isolated stablecoin usage. JupUSD also supports rewards during holding periods across multiple onchain features.
The rollout positions JupUSD as infrastructure rather than a standalone asset. Jupiter aligned issuance, routing, and incentives to strengthen onchain activity. As a result, JupUSD appears across web, mobile, and API surfaces as a default settlement asset.
Jupiter paired JupUSD with upgrades to lending and yield systems supporting stable demand. Jupiter Lend exited beta after rapid supply growth and released open source code. This environment allows JupUSD liquidity to move efficiently across borrowing, trading, and structured strategies.
Jupiter Lend uses tick-based liquidity built with Fluid for efficient liquidations. Consequently, the protocol offers higher loan ratios and lower penalties than comparable markets. These conditions

sources.title: Metaplex
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://metaplex.com/blog)

## October Highlights

- **Genesis**\- Genesis powered two presales in October, accounting for 18% of the total monthly revenue, the highest share since inception.

- **Protocol Revenue** \- Protocol revenue reached $2.0M in October.

- **$MPLX Buyback**\- $1.1M of Metaplex protocol fees were used to buy back 5.3M $MPLX (0.5% of total supply / 0.9% of outstanding supply) for the Metaplex DAO.


## Pump.Fun Update (November)

On November 7th, Pump.Fun [disclosed in its developer documentation](https://github.com/pump-fun/pump-public-docs/blob/main/README.md) that it would begin minting tokens using its new Mayhem Mode through SPL Token-22 rather than Metaplex鈥檚 Token Metadata. This change went into effect on November 12th and since then the percentage of total tokens launched daily by Pump.Fun that use Metaplex鈥檚 Token Metadata has declined to less than 15%.

Metaplex Foundation expects this to result in a decline in Metaplex protocol revenue near term with Pump.Fun contributing 44%, 60%, and 45% of total revenue during August, September, and October, respectively, after peaking at 86% in January 2025.

In light of the slowdown in memecoin activity, over the last few months the Metaplex Foundation has intentionally supported the expansion of the protocol from being purely an asset standard to a broader platform for Internet Capital Markets on Solana. Genesis, Metaplex鈥檚 protocol for fair onchain token launches, has found product market fit with over $1.1M in revenue generated since its launch in July 2025 and is now scaling. Metaplex is well positioned to become the canonical end-to-end platform for launching, discovering, and trading new assets on Solana.

The Metaplex Foundation remains well funded with more than three years of operating runway to support the Metaplex ecosystem even accounting for the loss of Pump.Fun associated revenue. This excludes the value of Foundation's and the Metaplex DAO's MPLX positions, which collectively account for more than 40% of the total supply. While this change will have a significant impact on revenue, the concentration risk was well understood, requiring no significant changes in the Metaplex Foundation鈥檚 operating plans or capital allocation.

Please note that the October revenue figures that follow in the rest of the newsletter below include revenue from Pump.Fun given the change occurred in November.

## October Protocol Activity

In October, Metaplex generated $2.0M in protocol revenue including $1.6M from Token Metadata (78%), $370K from Genesis (18%), and $72K from Core (4%). Genesis accounted for 18% of total revenue, its highest share since inception.

Combined, token creation (TM tokens) and token launches (Genesis) accounted for 91% of revenue while 9% was driven by the creation of NFTs (TM NFTs, Core, BG v2).

In October, 766K Token Metadata assets were created, including 720K fungible tokens (94%).

Core adoption increased in October, with 238K mints driving the cumulative mint total over 4M.

In October, creators and developers minted over 4M digital assets through Metaplex, including 3.0M compressed NFTs. This activity raised the protocol鈥檚 cumulative total to over 960M assets minted to date.

Unique user activity continued in October, with approximately 420K unique wallets authorizing transactions powered by Metaplex.Approximately 290K new wallets collected a Token Metadata asset and over 570K new wallets collected a Bubblegum NFT in the month of October.

## Developer Platform

**Genesis**

October marked another strong month for Genesis, powering multiple token launches and expanding its role across gaming, DeFi, and consumer apps.

[The month began with PlaySolana ($PLAY)](https://x.com/metaplex/status/1977434437748150707), a consumer gaming company building portable hardware devices and an onchain gaming ecosystem. [Their fixed-price presale sold out within minutes, selling over $2.6M (13.2K SOL) to more than 1,600 participant

tags.impact_reason: While the keynote discusses a critical aspect of AI security and its importance for the Solana ecosystem, its direct impact is still speculative and hinges on future adoption and integration.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline core encased in a perfect verification shell as the Semantic Anchor because it visually represents the core concept of protecting a complex, valuable AI model (the inner crystal) with a layer of trust and integrity (the outer shell). The story emphasizes the need for a 'shield' and 'verifiability' against AI corruption, and this high-tech, sacred geometry approach feels more conceptual and 'Wired'-esque than a literal shield. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'VERIFIED' as a holographic projection on the shell's surface. This is diegetic, directly communicates the product's main benefit, and enhances the futuristic, clinical mood.
visual_idea.description: In the center of a cavernous, dark room with polished obsidian floors, a complex, glowing crystalline neural network is suspended in mid-air. This inner core pulses with a soft, warm light, representing the AI model. Encasing this core is a perfectly transparent, geometrically precise outer shell made of a harder, clearer crystal, acting as the verification layer. The word 'VERIFIED' is projected holographically in a clean, minimalist font, its letters slowly tracing across the surface of the outer shell, confirming the integrity of the core within. The background is deep and out-of-focus, hinting at server racks with faint data lights, creating a sense of scale and placing the object in a high-security data vault.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A towering, monumental crystalline structure dominates a cavernous, dark room, composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio with ample negative space above, emphasizing epic scale. In the center, a complex, glowing crystalline neural network is suspended in mid-air, pulsing with a soft, warm light that represents the AI model. Encasing this core is a perfectly transparent, geometrically precise outer shell made of a harder, clearer crystal, acting as the verification layer. The word 'VERIFIED' is projected holographically in a clean, minimalist font, its letters slowly tracing across the surface of the outer shell, confirming the integrity of the core within. The polished obsidian floors below reflect the glowing network and holographic text with extreme clarity, creating intricate refractive caustics and deep, glossy reflections. The background is deep and out-of-focus, hinting at server racks with faint data lights, creating a sense of scale and placing the object in a high-security data vault, evoking a high-tech relic. Dramatic, cinematic lighting accentuates the crystal's clarity, the obsidian's sheen, and the ethereal glow of the neural network. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, deep shadows, volumetric lighting, complex refractions, glossy reflections, monumental scale, 3/4 aspect ratio.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline core encased in a perfect verification shell as the Semantic Anchor because it visually represents the core concept of protecting a complex, valuable AI model (the inner crystal) with a layer of trust and integrity (the outer shell). The story emphasizes the need for a 'shield' and 'verifiability' against AI corruption, and this high-tech, sacred geometry approach feels more conceptual and 'Wired'-esque than a literal shield. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'VERIFIED' as a holographic projection on the shell's surface. This is diegetic, directly communicates the product's main benefit, and enhances the futuristic, clinical mood.
visual_idea.description: A macro, detailed shot focusing on the precise interface between the inner, glowing crystal core and the perfectly clear, geometric outer shell. We see the intricate, glowing filaments and pathways of the inner AI model meeting the flawless, hard edge of the protective verification layer. The light from the core refracts beautifully through the outer shell, creating a rainbow of prismatic effects and sharp caustics on its facets. The complexity of the inner structure contrasts powerfully with the purity and perfect structure of the outer casing.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, detailed shot focusing on the precise interface between the inner, glowing crystal core and the perfectly clear, geometric outer shell. We see the intricate, glowing filaments and pathways of the inner AI model meeting the flawless, hard edge of the protective verification layer. The light from the core refracts beautifully through the outer shell, creating a rainbow of prismatic effects and sharp caustics on its facets. The complexity of the inner structure contrasts powerfully with the purity and perfect structure of the outer casing. The geometric outer shell possesses the mirror-like, ultra-polished reflectivity of obsidian, with flawless, hard edges and a pristine, high-tech relic aesthetic. The lighting is soft commercial product lighting, utilizing global illumination and a large diffused softbox for neutral white (5500K) illumination and a white reflector to soften shadows, ensuring even light distribution and subtle ambient occlusion in all creases. The composition is symmetrical and centrally focused, filling the square frame (1:1 aspect ratio) with intricate textures and hyper-detailed surfaces. Photorealistic, hyperrealistic detail, V6 realism, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range. Captured with a studio macro lens, 100mm telephoto, at an f/16 aperture for deep depth of field and edge-to-edge sharpness. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR).

editorial.headline: Ambient Delivers Verified AI Inference at Standard Cost Parity
editorial.standfirst: The a16z-backed protocol targets the "Agentic Economy" with a verification layer that eliminates the asymmetric risk of optimistic models. Founder Travis Good positions the tech as a necessary shield for AI agents managing institutional capital.
key_takeaways: **Economic Parity:** Ambient offers cryptographically verified inference at the same price as standard unverified compute, removing the 10x-1000x cost penalty of traditional ZK methods.
**Infrastructure:** The protocol is live with support for high-scale models like **GLM 4.6** (16-bit quantization), utilizing TEEs strictly for privacy rather than security.
**Market Context:** Good cited recent exploits in **Anthropic鈥檚 Claude Code** inference engine to demonstrate why "optimistic" security models fail for high-value agentic workloads.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The transition to an agentic economy鈥攚here AI models execute transactions and manage wallets鈥攈as hit a critical bottleneck: trust. As **Ambient** founder Travis Good argued at Breakpoint, the industry鈥檚 current reliance on "optimistic" verification creates an unacceptable asymmetric risk. If an agent can be manipulated to earn $10 million while the slashing penalty is only $10,000, the security model collapses. Good pointed to the recent compromise of **Anthropic鈥檚 Claude Code** inference engine as a bellwether event, demonstrating that even top-tier providers are vulnerable to infrastructure-level exploits that can render agents "suddenly stupid" or adversarial.

Ambient鈥檚 solution is a technical pivot from the two prevailing standards: Zero-Knowledge (ZK) proofs and Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). While ZK remains prohibitively expensive (often 10-1000x the cost of raw inference) and TEEs have historically suffered from side-channel compromises, Ambient introduces a verification layer that operates at the exact price point of unverified inference. By decoupling privacy (handled via TEEs) from security (handled via verified inference), the protocol guarantees that every token and pixel is rendered correctly by the specified model, such as the **GLM 4.6** showcased in their live demo.

For institutional investors and developers, this unlocks "provably fair economic games" on **Solana**. Currently, on-chain AI apps largely rely on trusting the model provider not to front-run or manipulate outputs. Ambient鈥檚 infrastructure鈥攂acked by **a16z CSX** and **Delphi Digital**鈥攁llows developers to integrate search, deep research, and chat capabilities with cryptographic certainty. This commoditization of trust suggests a shift where verified inference becomes the minimum standard for DeFi-integrated agents, rather than a premium luxury.
